mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment directly reduces consumer income and spending, leading to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 5-day returns show no significant negative correlation with UNRATE changes over any 2-year window, or rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, monetary policy is tight enough to compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, predicting negative returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% over any 3-year window, or the signal is not robust to alternative thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and rise in unemployment signals severe demand contraction, causing corporate profits to fall and S&P 500 returns to be negative over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline > 0.5% and UNRATE month-over-month increase > 0.2%
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, forward 60-day returns are not significantly different from zero or do not outperform a baseline using each series individually.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production forecast negative S&P 500 returns, with the effect amplified during NBER-defined recessions as supply constraints worsen.
failure_condition: The negative predictive power of INDPRO declines is not significantly stronger during recessions than expansions (paired test p > 0.10) or the regime effect fails out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts reduce construction activity and consumer wealth, leading to lower economic growth and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST changes show no significant relationship with forward 60-day returns over any 5-year period, or rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 100,000 signals acute labor market distress, which reduces aggregate demand and depresses S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative when PAYEMS declines exceed 100,000, or the signal is not persistent across multiple recessionary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation is elevated simultaneously with high federal funds rates, financial conditions tighten sharply and real earnings expectations fall, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The joint condition does not produce negative 5-day returns with a mean return significantly below zero, or the interaction term in a regression of returns on both series is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarterly GDP growth signals a contraction in aggregate output, lowering future cash flow expectations and driving S&P 500 returns negative over the next quarter, especially when growth is below zero.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not significantly lower after negative GDP growth quarters compared to positive growth quarters, with a t-test p-value greater than 0.05.
